From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American television writer and producer

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

Goodman"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22David+H.+Goodman%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22David+H.+Goodman%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22David+H.+Goodman%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22David+H.+Goodman%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22David+H.+Goodman%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22David+H.+Goodman%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2012)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| David H. Goodman | |
| --- | --- |
| Goodman at [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") in July 2017 | |
| Other names | David Goodman |
| Occupations | Writer, producer |

**David H. Goodman** is an American [television writer](/wiki/Television_writer "Television writer") and [producer](/wiki/Television_producer "Television producer").

He is known for his work on the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") [drama](/wiki/Drama_(genre) "Drama (genre)") *[Without a Trace](/wiki/Without_a_Trace "Without a Trace")* and the [FOX](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Network "Fox Broadcasting Network") [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*. He most recently served as writer and executive producer on the [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_(genre) "Fantasy (genre)") series *[Once Upon a Time](/wiki/Once_Upon_a_Time_(TV_series) "Once Upon a Time (TV series)")* from 2011-2018.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_H._Goodman&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

In 1997, Goodman became a script coordinator for the [Sci-Fi](/wiki/Syfy "Syfy") television series *[The Visitor](/wiki/The_Visitor_(TV_series) "The Visitor (TV series)")*. The series was cancelled after its first season and Goodman became script coordinator for the supernatural drama series *[Buffy the Vampire Slayer](/wiki/Buffy_the_Vampire_Slayer_(TV_series) "Buffy the Vampire Slayer (TV series)")*. He joined the crew of *Buffy* mid-way through the second season in 1998. Goodman left the show with the close of the sixth season in 2002.

While working on *Buffy,* his writing debut was for Bruce Campbell vehicle *[Jack of All Trades](/wiki/Jack_of_All_Trades_(TV_series) "Jack of All Trades (TV series)")* with the second season episode "Croquey in the Pokey" in 2001. He wrote two episodes for the third season of *[Angel](/wiki/Angel_(1999_TV_series) "Angel (1999 TV series)")* – "Dad" in 2001 and "Double or Nothing" in 2002.

In 2002, he became a story editor and writer for action series *[Fastlane](/wiki/Fastlane_(TV_series) "Fastlane (TV series)")*, scripting the first season episode "Dosed". He also wrote for the [Batman](/wiki/Batman "Batman") spin-off *[Birds of Prey](/wiki/Birds_of_Prey_(TV_series) "Birds of Prey (TV series)")* and contributed two episodes, "Three Birds and a Baby" in 2002 and "Gladiatrix" in 2003.

In 2003, he became involved with *[Without a Trace](/wiki/Without_a_Trace "Without a Trace")* as a story editor for the second season. He wrote the second season episodes "Moving On" (2003) and "Risen" (2004). He became an executive story editor for the third season and contributed two more episodes "Trials" (2004) and "Transitions" (2005). He joined the production team for the fourth season, initially as a co-producer. He wrote the episodes "Lost Time" and "Odds or Evens". He was promoted to producer mid-season and wrote a third episode, "Shattered". He became a supervising producer for the fifth season and wrote the episodes "Win Today" (2006), "Eating Away" (2007), "Connections" (2007) and the season finale "The Beginning" (2007). He became a co-executive producer for the sixth season and wrote the episodes "Fight/Flight" (2007) and "A Dollar and a Dream" (2008). He left the show in Spring 2008 having written thirteen episodes in total.

Goodman is a 1995 graduate of [Wesleyan University](/wiki/Wesleyan_University "Wesleyan University").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### *Fringe*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_H._Goodman&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Fringe")]

In 2008, he joined the crew on the [first season](/wiki/Fringe_(season_1) "Fringe (season 1)") of the [FOX](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Network "Fox Broadcasting Network") [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") [thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(genre) "Thriller (genre)") *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*, as a writer and co-executive producer. After completing the season, Goodman left the series. He, along with the rest of the writing staff, was nominated for the [Writers Guild of America Award](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Awards "Writers Guild of America Awards") for Best New Series at the [February 2009 ceremony](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Awards_2008 "Writers Guild of America Awards 2008") for his work on the first season.[[2]](#cite_note-WGA_09_Official-2) Episodes Goodman co-wrote include:

* "[The Ghost Network](/wiki/The_Ghost_Network "The Ghost Network")" (1.03) (co-written by supervising producer [J.R. Orci](/wiki/J.R._Orci "J.R. Orci"))
* "[The Equation](/wiki/The_Equation "The Equation")" (1.08) (co-written by Orci)
* "[Safe](/wiki/Safe_(Fringe) "Safe (Fringe)")" (1.08) (co-written by consulting producer [Jason Cahill](/wiki/Jason_Cahill "Jason Cahill"))
* "[The No-Brainer](/wiki/The_No-Brainer "The No-Brainer")" (1.12) (co-written by co-producer [Brad Caleb Kane](/wiki/Brad_Caleb_Kane "Brad Caleb Kane"))
* "[Ability](/wiki/Ability_(Fringe) "Ability (Fringe)")" (1.14) (Goodman wrote a teleplay, based on a story by executive story editors [Robert Chiappetta](/wiki/Robert_Chiappetta "Robert Chiappetta") and [Glen Whitman](/wiki/Glen_Whitman "Glen Whitman"))
* "[Unearthed](/wiki/Unearthed_(Fringe) "Unearthed (Fringe)")" (2.11) (co-written by co-executive producer [Andrew Kreisberg](/wiki/Andrew_Kreisberg "Andrew Kreisberg"))

### *Once Upon a Time*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_H._Goodman&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Once Upon a Time")]

In Summer 2011, Goodman was brought on to the [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_(genre) "Fantasy (genre)") [drama](/wiki/Drama_(genre) "Drama (genre)") *[Once Upon a Time](/wiki/Once_Upon_a_Time_(TV_series) "Once Upon a Time (TV series)")*, as a writer and co-executive producer.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Episodes Goodman has contributed to include:

* "[The Price of Gold](/wiki/The_Price_of_Gold "The Price of Gold")" 1.04
* "[True North](/wiki/True_North_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "True North (Once Upon a Time)")" 01.09 (co-written with [Liz Tigelaar](/wiki/Liz_Tigelaar "Liz Tigelaar"))
* "[What Happened to Frederick](/wiki/What_Happened_to_Frederick "What Happened to Frederick")" 1.13
* "[Hat Trick](/wiki/Hat_Trick "Hat Trick")" 1.17 (co-written with Vladimir Cvetko)
* "[An Apple Red as Blood](/wiki/An_Apple_Red_as_Blood "An Apple Red as Blood")" 1.21 (co-written with [Jane Espenson](/wiki/Jane_Espenson "Jane Espenson"))
* "[The Crocodile](/wiki/The_Crocodile_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "The Crocodile (Once Upon a Time)")" 2.04 (co-written with [Robert Hull](/wiki/Robert_Hull "Robert Hull"))
* "[The Cricket Game](/wiki/The_Cricket_Game "The Cricket Game")" 2.10 (co-written with [Robert Hull](/wiki/Robert_Hull "Robert Hull"))
* "[The Queen is Dead](/wiki/The_Queen_is_Dead_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "The Queen is Dead (Once Upon a Time)")" 2.15 (co-written with [Daniel T. Thomsen](/wiki/Daniel_T._Thomsen "Daniel T. Thomsen"))
* "[Nasty Habits](/wiki/Nasty_Habits_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Nasty Habits (Once Upon a Time)")" 3.04 (co-written with Robert Hull)
* "[Think Lovely Thoughts](/wiki/Think_Lovely_Thoughts "Think Lovely Thoughts")" 3.08 (co-written with Robert Hull)
* "[The Jolly Roger](/wiki/The_Jolly_Roger_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "The Jolly Roger (Once Upon a Time)")" 3.17
* "[Snow Drifts](/wiki/Snow_Drifts "Snow Drifts")" 3.21 (co-written with Robert Hull)
* "[Rocky Road](/wiki/Rocky_Road_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Rocky Road (Once Upon a Time)")" 4.03 (co-written with [Jerome Schwartz](/wiki/Jerome_Schwartz "Jerome Schwartz"))
* "[Smash the Mirror](/wiki/Smash_the_Mirror "Smash the Mirror")" 4.08 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[Enter the Dragon](/wiki/Enter_the_Dragon_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Enter the Dragon (Once Upon a Time)")" 4.14 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[Sympathy for the De Vil](/wiki/Sympathy_for_the_De_Vil "Sympathy for the De Vil")" 4.18 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[The Broken Kingdom](/wiki/The_Broken_Kingdom "The Broken Kingdom")" 5.04 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[Birth](/wiki/Birth_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Birth (Once Upon a Time)")" 5.08 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[The Brothers Jones](/wiki/The_Brothers_Jones "The Brothers Jones")" 5.15 (co-written with Jerome Schwartz)
* "[Sisters](/wiki/Sisters_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Sisters (Once Upon a Time)")" 5.19 (co-written with Brigitte Hales)
* "[Only You](/wiki/Only_You_(Once_Upon_a_Time) "Only You (Once Upon a Time)")" 5.22 (co-written with [Andrew Chambliss](/wiki/Andrew_Chambliss "Andrew Chambliss"))
* "[Strange Case](/wiki/Strange_Case "Strange Case")" 6.04 (co-written with Nelson Soler)
* "[Changelings](/wiki/Changelings "Changelings")" 6.09 (co-written with Brian Ridings)
* "[Page 23](/wiki/Page_23 "Page 23")" 6.14 (co-written with Brigitte Hales)
* "[Where Bluebirds Fly](/wiki/Where_Bluebirds_Fly "Where Bluebirds Fly")" 6.18 (co-written with Brigitte Hales)
* "[The Song in Your Heart](/wiki/The_Song_in_Your_Heart "The Song in Your Heart")" 6.20 (co-written with Andrew Chambliss)
* "The Garden of Forking Paths" 7.03 (co-written with Brigitte Hales)
